text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy rural town, there lived a quirky old man named Mr. Jenkins. He had a garden full of flowers unlike any other. The color pink dominated—blooming vibrant roses in spring and blushes of cherry blossoms in autumn.

Every morning, Mr. Jenkins would frolic among his flowers, humming tunes from an uncertain time. His favorite was "Moon River." He believed the tunes could talk.

One summer day, rain drizzled gently while he watered his roses. Out of nowhere, a strange bird landed beside him. Its feathers were iridescent shades of blue and green.

Mr. Jenkins named it Sylvie.

Sylvie fluttered around talking to nothing in particular. She sang an old song Mr. Jenkins knew by heart. Each word Sylvie sang seemed to cut through clouds.

Days turned into weeks, and Sylvie became Mr. Jenkins' best company. She'd follow him everywhere. In the orchard, she'd perch atop
